strengths: Clearly lists Java SDK version requirement (>= 22.0.1)
Provides the correct Docker command to start httpbin mock
Step-by-step numbered structure is easy to follow
weaknesses: Only documents 1 of 3 system components — no mention of Node.js relay or Vue.js client
IntelliJ-specific instructions exclude users of other IDEs or CLI-only environments
No project overview or purpose statement anywhere in the file
No architecture description or component relationship explanation
Does not mention Maven as a build tool
No dependency versions for GlassFish Tyrus, javax.json, or any other Java library
Content Root path references 'websocket_swing/swing/src/main/java' with a leading prefix not matching the actual repo structure
No troubleshooting section
No link to Node server or Vue client documentation
Missing: what the application actually does

strengths: Provides the correct docker run command
weaknesses: Placed deep in the source tree — not discoverable
Contains 'don't ask why' — removes learning opportunity and signals undocumented dependency
Duplicates the Docker command already in root README without adding context
No explanation of WHY this container is needed (httpbin mock backend for HTTP POST)
No mention of port mapping rationale or what happens if httpbin is unavailable
Not linked from root README

strengths: Lists standard yarn commands for development workflow
Links to Vue CLI configuration reference
weaknesses: 100% auto-generated Vue CLI boilerplate — no project-specific content added
Does not describe what this component does
Does not mention WebSocket connection to Node.js relay
No mention of port used by dev server
No description of the Search component or the person-transfer feature
No Node.js or yarn version requirements
yarn run test command does not work (no test suite configured)
No mention of Vue 2 (EOL December 2023)

strengths: Shows the command to serve the Vue client locally
weaknesses: Contains a hardcoded personal Windows path (C:/Users/esultano/...) — not portable
Vuetify install/uninstall instructions are commented-out history, not instructions
Plain .txt format — lacks any structure
Hidden in doc/ subfolder — not discoverable via root navigation
Global @vue/cli install instruction may conflict with project's local CLI version
No description of what the client does or how it connects to the backend

strengths: Shows the correct node command to start the WebSocket server
weaknesses: Contains a hardcoded personal Windows path (C:/Users/esultano/...) — not portable
export NODE_PATH instruction is a Windows Bash (Git Bash) hack — not cross-platform
npm install -g websocket suggests global install; actual dependency is local (node-server/package.json)
No description of what the WebSocket server does
No mention of port 1337 or how clients connect to it
Plain .txt format — no structure
Not linked from root README

strengths: Uses OpenAPI 3.0.1 — a standard machine-readable format
Documents all 13 PostObject field names matching ModelProperties enum
Provides request body and response schemas
weaknesses: Only documents the httpbin mock /post endpoint — not the actual system API
Endpoint is a mock echo service, not a real business endpoint; spec does not clarify this
Response schema wraps PostObject in a 'postObject' property, but actual httpbin response returns it in 'json' field — schema mismatch
All field types are 'string' — IBAN, BIC, DATE_OF_BIRTH, ZIP should have format constraints
No field descriptions — FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE, ORT, IBAN provide no context to readers unfamiliar with German
WebSocket endpoint (ws://localhost:1337) not documented at all
No error response schemas (4xx, 5xx)
No authentication documentation
No example values for any field
description field in info object is minimal
No contact, license, or termsOfService fields

critical_gaps.gap: No system overview or project purpose documentation
critical_gaps.impact: A new developer has no starting point — the repository looks like a Java Swing project with unknown purpose
critical_gaps.recommendation: Add a project overview section to README.md explaining: what Allegro is, what the PoC demonstrates, the 3-component architecture, and the German business context

critical_gaps.gap: Node.js WebSocket relay server has zero user-facing documentation
critical_gaps.impact: The central middleware component is invisible to anyone reading the documentation; the system cannot be started without it
critical_gaps.recommendation: Create node-server/README.md documenting: purpose, port (1337), start command, WebSocket message format {target, content}, client connection URL, and the broadcast relay behaviour

critical_gaps.impact: Integration with any client is impossible without reading the source code; the main real-time synchronisation feature is invisible
critical_gaps.recommendation: Document WebSocket API: connection endpoint, message schema {target: string, content: string}, broadcast semantics, and client lifecycle events

critical_gaps.gap: No end-to-end startup guide covering all three components
critical_gaps.impact: A developer must piece together startup instructions from 4 separate files, two of which contain personal file paths
critical_gaps.recommendation: Add a Quick Start section to README.md with ordered steps: (1) start httpbin, (2) start Node WebSocket server, (3) start Vue client, (4) run Java Swing app, with all required ports listed

critical_gaps.gap: Vue.js person search and Allegro transfer feature is not documented anywhere
critical_gaps.impact: The primary user-facing workflow — searching for a person in the browser and transferring to the Allegro desktop application — is completely undocumented
critical_gaps.recommendation: Document the Search component: purpose, how to search persons, what Transfer to Allegro does, and the real-time sync effect on the Swing application

major_gaps.gap: No architecture documentation — how the three components communicate is undocumented
major_gaps.impact: Developers cannot understand data flow or system design without reading 15 source files
major_gaps.recommendation: Add architecture section to README.md with component diagram, protocol details (HTTP vs WebSocket), and port reference table

major_gaps.gap: German field names (ORT, PLZ, Vorname, etc.) have no English translations or explanations
major_gaps.impact: Non-German-speaking developers cannot understand the domain model fields in api.yml or the UI
major_gaps.recommendation: Add field descriptions to api.yml for all 13 fields with English translations and domain context

major_gaps.gap: Real-time textarea synchronisation feature is not documented
major_gaps.impact: One of the 3 major workflows is invisible in the documentation
major_gaps.recommendation: Document in README.md: how the Vue textarea syncs in real-time to the Swing form via WebSocket broadcast

major_gaps.gap: Java dependency versions not documented (GlassFish Tyrus, javax.json, WebSocket API)
major_gaps.impact: Developers cannot verify their environment or troubleshoot classpath issues
major_gaps.recommendation: Add a dependencies section or table to README.md listing all pom.xml dependencies with versions

major_gaps.gap: api.yml response schema does not match actual httpbin response structure
major_gaps.impact: Developers relying on the OpenAPI spec will write incorrect response parsing code
major_gaps.affected_field: PostResponseObject.json vs actual httpbin json field wrapping
major_gaps.recommendation: Update PostResponseObject to accurately reflect the httpbin echo response, including the 'json' property wrapping

major_gaps.gap: No contributing guidelines or development workflow documentation
major_gaps.impact: New contributors have no guidance on branching, testing (or lack thereof), or PR process
major_gaps.recommendation: Add CONTRIBUTING.md or a Contributing section to README.md

major_gaps.impact: Teams may plan extensions on an unsupported framework without realising the risk
major_gaps.recommendation: Add a note in node-vue-client/README.md: Vue 2 reached EOL December 2023; migration to Vue 3 is recommended for production

minor_gaps.recommendation: Add common issues: Java version mismatch, Docker not running, port conflicts on 1337/8080, WebSocket connection failures

minor_gaps.gap: No license file or license statement
minor_gaps.recommendation: Add LICENSE file or license declaration to README.md
Document minimum Node.js version in node-server/README.md and node-vue-client/README.md

minor_gaps.gap: yarn vs npm inconsistency across components
minor_gaps.recommendation: Standardise on one package manager and document it
Add CHANGELOG.md noting this is a PoC at version 0.0.1-SNAPSHOT
Document in README.md what the .launch file is (IntelliJ run configuration) and how to use it

minor_gaps.gap: No inline code documentation (Javadoc, JSDoc) in any source file
minor_gaps.recommendation: Add at minimum public API Javadoc to PocPresenter, PocModel, EventEmitter, HttpBinService

implemented_endpoints: WebSocket message {target, content} — inbound from Vue client
WebSocket broadcast — outbound to all connected clients
missing_in_docs: WebSocket message protocol {target: string, content: string}

documented_configs: Port 8080 (README.md via docker command, api.yml server URL)

undocumented_features.feature: Transfer to Allegro workflow via WebSocket
MVP pattern in Java Swing layer
Mock person dataset (5 hardcoded persons in Search.vue)

documented_but_broken.issue: No test suite configured in package.json — command will fail

architecture_comparison.documented_architecture: Single-component — only Java Swing setup described. No system architecture documented.
architecture_comparison.actual_architecture: Three-component MVP polyglot system: Java Swing desktop (MVP) + Node.js WebSocket relay (port 1337) + Vue.js SPA browser client, communicating via HTTP (port 8080) and WebSocket (port 1337)

summary: The Allegro PoC has critically insufficient documentation for a multi-component system. Of the 6 documentation artifacts present, only the OpenAPI spec (api.yml) provides structured technical value — and even it has a schema mismatch and is missing WebSocket API coverage entirely. The root README documents just one of three components. The Node.js WebSocket relay server — the architectural centrepiece connecting browser and desktop — has zero user-facing documentation. Two doc files contain personal Windows file paths from the original developer, making them actively harmful for other users. The core features (person search, Allegro transfer, real-time sync) are never mentioned in any documentation file. Overall documentation score: 2.5/10 — Inadequate. An estimated 14 hours of documentation work would raise this to Adequate (6/10).
